title: Regex: движок, RegexOptions.Compiled и GeneratedRegex
note: Sandbox does not reference System.Text.RegularExpressions at compile time, so card code drives the genuine runtime Regex engine via reflection (Type.GetType + InvokeMember). Output is real Regex behavior; expectedOutput matches the natural-API question shown to the learner. [GeneratedRegex] source generators run at project compile time, not in CSharpScript, so they are taught conceptually and the runtime behavior is proven with new Regex / RegexOptions.Compiled.

cards.prompt: new Regex(@"\d{3}-\d{4}").IsMatch on "call 555-1234 now" is True, and Match(...).Value returns only the matched substring 555-1234, printing True then 555-1234
cards.code: using System; using System.Reflection; var rt = Type.GetType("System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex, System.Text.RegularExpressions"); var re = Activator.CreateInstance(rt, new object[]{@"\d{3}-\d{4}"}); Console.WriteLine((bool)rt.GetMethod("IsMatch", new[]{typeof(string)}).Invoke(re, new object[]{"call 555-1234 now"})); var m = rt.GetMethod("Match", new[]{typeof(string)}).Invoke(re, new object[]{"call 555-1234 now"}); Console.WriteLine((string)m.GetType().GetProperty("Value").GetValue(m));

cards.prompt: new Regex(@"\w+", RegexOptions.Compiled).Matches("one two three") finds 3 matches and ms[2].Value is three; RegexOptions.Compiled changes the engine (IL via reflection-emit) not the result, printing 3 then three
cards.code: using System; using System.Reflection; var rt = Type.GetType("System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex, System.Text.RegularExpressions"); var ot = Type.GetType("System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions, System.Text.RegularExpressions"); var compiled = Enum.Parse(ot, "Compiled"); var re = Activator.CreateInstance(rt, new object[]{@"\w+", compiled}); var ms = rt.GetMethod("Matches", new[]{typeof(string)}).Invoke(re, new object[]{"one two three"}); var mst = ms.GetType(); Console.WriteLine((int)mst.GetProperty("Count").GetValue(ms)); var item = mst.GetProperty("Item", new[]{typeof(int)}).GetValue(ms, new object[]{2}); Console.WriteLine((string)item.GetType().GetProperty("Value").GetValue(item));

cards.prompt: new Regex(@"(\w+)@(\w+)", RegexOptions.IgnoreCase) matched against "USER@Example rest" captures Groups[1]=USER and Groups[2]=Example, and Replace("a@b c@d", "X") replaces each match giving X X, printing USER, Example, X X
cards.code: using System; using System.Reflection; var rt = Type.GetType("System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex, System.Text.RegularExpressions"); var ot = Type.GetType("System.Text.RegularExpressions.RegexOptions, System.Text.RegularExpressions"); var ic = Enum.Parse(ot, "IgnoreCase"); var re = Activator.CreateInstance(rt, new object[]{@"(\w+)@(\w+)", ic}); var m = rt.GetMethod("Match", new[]{typeof(string)}).Invoke(re, new object[]{"USER@Example rest"}); var groups = m.GetType().GetProperty("Groups").GetValue(m); var gt = groups.GetType(); var g1 = gt.GetProperty("Item", new[]{typeof(int)}).GetValue(groups, new object[]{1}); var g2 = gt.GetProperty("Item", new[]{typeof(int)}).GetValue(groups, new object[]{2}); Console.WriteLine((string)g1.GetType().GetProperty("Value").GetValue(g1)); Console.WriteLine((string)g2.GetType().GetProperty("Value").GetValue(g2)); var rep = rt.GetMethod("Replace", new[]{typeof(string), typeof(string)}).Invoke(re, new object[]{"a@b c@d", "X"}); Console.WriteLine((string)rep);
